services.description:
NTCIP 1205 CCTV / PTZ camera management — the neutral field-device
model: identity and mounting, PTZ position / presets / tours,
stream/encoder status, enclosure environmental controls, and an
active-fault inventory. Stream/encoder status is the field-device side
(what the camera/encoder emits), distinct from a center-side VMS.

Vendor analytics calibration (e.g. TrafficVision detection-zone
calibration) is NOT camera management and stays in the vendor augments;
this is the neutral device model those augments extend. Joystick-
ownership arbitration (who holds control) lands in a follow-up revision.

Copyright (c) 2026. Distributed under Apache 2.0.

YANG data model for Dynamic Message Signs (DMS).

Transport-independent replacement for NTCIP 1203 v03, aligned
with ARC-IT 9.1 service package TI06 (Traffic Information
Dissemination).  The model is deliberately structure-first: sign
identity, a message-memory library, a commanded control surface
(mode, brightness, message activation, and fallback messages),
environment sensors, and pixel/lamp health live under a single
`sign` container with config / state separation applied to every
node that has both sides.

Nodes are annotated with the 'arc-it-flow' extension so tooling
can emit ARC-IT coverage reports.

Copyright (c) 2026.  Distributed under Apache 2.0.
refStd: ARC-IT 9.1 Service Package TI06 (Traffic Information Dissemination)
NTCIP 1203 v03 (functional reference only; no wire compatibility)
RFC 7950 section 11 (breaking YANG evolution: identity rename)

RFC 7950 section 11 (breaking YANG evolution: identity rename)

services.description:
YANG data model for Environmental Sensor Stations (ESS) — the
roadside devices commonly referred to as RWIS (Road Weather
Information System).

Transport-independent replacement for NTCIP 1204 v04, aligned
with ARC-IT 9.1 service package MC01 (Environmental Monitoring
Station).

ESS devices are pure-observation: no actuators, no commands, no
safety-critical timing.  The model therefore has almost no
`must`-constraints.  Bounded values are enforced via YANG
`range` (humidity 0..100 %, wind-direction 0..360°, pressure
300..1100 hPa, etc.) rather than XPath constraints, because the
bounds are **physical** rather than **operational**.

Every numeric leaf carries a `units` statement.  Agents that
report different units MUST convert at the translator boundary;
the wire format is canonical.

Copyright (c) 2026.  Distributed under Apache 2.0.
refStd: ARC-IT 9.1 Service Package MC01 (Environmental Monitoring Station)
NTCIP 1204 v04 (functional reference only; no wire compatibility)
RFC 7950 section 11 (breaking YANG evolution: identity rename)

modules.name: openits-ess
refStd: ARC-IT 9.1 Service Package MC01 (Environmental Monitoring Station)
NTCIP 1204 v04 (functional reference only; no wire compatibility)
RFC 7950 section 11 (breaking YANG evolution: identity rename)

modules.name: openits-ess-events
refStd: RFC 7950 section 11 (breaking YANG evolution: identity rename)

services.description:
YANG data model for roadside perception sensors — LiDAR and
tracking-radar units that emit object-level tracks (position,
speed, heading, classification) rather than per-lane aggregates.

Aligned with ARC-IT 9.1 service packages TM01 (Infrastructure-
Based Traffic Surveillance) and TM08 (Traffic Incident
Management System).  The model is vendor-neutral: it applies to
any sensor whose on-board perception pipeline produces tracked
objects and zone analytics.  There is no NTCIP analog — this is
a post-NTCIP device class.  Vendor-specific capabilities ride
in vendor augment modules per docs/06-extension-model.md, never
in this module.

Object classes and incident types are identity hierarchies, not
enums: perception vendors extend both via the identity-only
Tier-2 pattern (docs/06-extension-model.md) without touching
this module.  Closed physical sets remain enums.

Track lists are high-churn state; consumers needing full-rate
object data subscribe to the streamed state path rather than
polling.  Zone analytics and incidents are the operator-facing
product and ride as notifications.

Copyright (c) 2026.  Distributed under Apache 2.0.
refStd: ARC-IT 9.1 Service Packages TM01 (Infrastructure-Based Traffic Surveillance) and TM08 (Traffic Incident Management System)
RFC 7950 section 11 (breaking YANG evolution: identity rename)

modules.name: openits-perception
refStd: ARC-IT 9.1 Service Packages TM01 (Infrastructure-Based Traffic Surveillance) and TM08 (Traffic Incident Management System)
RFC 7950 section 11 (breaking YANG evolution: identity rename)

modules.name: openits-perception-events
refStd: RFC 7950 section 11 (breaking YANG evolution: identity rename)

services.description:
YANG data model for freeway entrance-ramp metering devices.

Transport-independent replacement for NTCIP 1207 v02, aligned
with ARC-IT 9.1 service package TI03 (Traffic Metering).

Ramp meters share NEMA phase-timing vocabulary with signal
controllers; the shared grouping lives in openits-nema-common
so the MUTCD minimums stay in one place across services.
Ramp-meter-specific constructs (release rate, headway, queue
override, per-lane operation) are modelled here.

Copyright (c) 2026.  Distributed under Apache 2.0.
refStd: NTCIP 1207 v02 (functional reference only; no wire compatibility)
NTCIP 1207:2017 (ramp-meter functional reference; fault classes, metering modes)
RFC 7950 section 11 (breaking YANG evolution: identity rename)

services.description:
YANG data model for reversible-lane facilities — corridors,
bridges, and tunnels whose lanes carry traffic in either of two
opposing directions under operator control.

Aligned with ARC-IT 9.1 service package TM16 (Reversible Lane
Management).  Facilities of this class are typically driven by
a PLC exposing lane state through a register map; the model is
transport-independent, so SRTP, Modbus, DNP3, or NTCIP adapters
map onto it identically.  Wire provenance for register-decoded
events rides in the openits-types wire-source 'plc-register'
case.  Vendor-specific capabilities ride in vendor augment
modules per docs/06-extension-model.md, never in this module.

The facility is a four-state machine.  With direction-a =
northbound and direction-b = southbound, the operator-visible
states map to: Open NB (open + northbound), Open SB (open +
southbound), Closed, and In-Transition.  Directions are
configured per facility so east-west corridors use the same
model.

Structured per the config/state idiom: every commanded surface
(facility `config` directions, `control/config`, and each lane's
`segments/segment/lane/config`) is config true and every
readback (`state`, `control/state`, and each lane's
`segments/segment/lane/state`) is config false, one per lane per
MUTCD Chapter 4M (a lane-use control signal governs a single
lane, not a whole segment).

This is a safety-critical control model.  `must` constraints on
every commanded, config-true surface forbid unsafe or
inconsistent writes and conforming controllers MUST refuse
writes that violate them:
  - the facility's two travel directions must be concrete and
    cardinal opposites (`config/direction-a` /
    `config/direction-b`);
  - an open target-state must name a target-direction that is
    one of the facility's configured directions
    (`control/config`);
  - per lane, a commanded green-arrow or flashing-yellow-X
    toward one direction requires a commanded steady red-X
    toward the other (`segments/segment/lane/config`) — the
    green-implies-opposing-red-X invariant.
The config-false readback (`state`, `control/state`,
`segments/segment/lane/state`) carries no `must`: it reports
anomalies — including a lane reading back green-arrow to both
directions — as data plus a fault/alarm, not as illegal data;
a misbehaving facility's telemetry must stay representable so
it can be reported (see lcs-conflict-detected).

Copyright (c) 2026.  Distributed under Apache 2.0.
refStd: ARC-IT 9.1 Service Package TM16 (Reversible Lane Management)
RFC 7950 section 11 (breaking YANG evolution: identity rename)

refStd: ARC-IT 9.1 Service Package TM16 (Reversible Lane Management)
RFC 7950 section 11 (breaking YANG evolution: identity rename)

services.description:
Roadside Unit (RSU) YANG module for V2X communication.
Models NTCIP 1218-compliant RSUs including DSRC/C-V2X channels,
V2X message broadcasting (SPaT, MAP, TIM; BSM receive/forward), and
security.

services.description:
A YANG data model for roadside traffic signal controllers.

This module is designed to align with the ARC-IT (Architecture
Reference for Cooperative and Intelligent Transportation)
Traffic Signal Control service package, providing a transport-
independent replacement for the SNMP/MIB coupling of NTCIP 1202.

Nodes are annotated with the 'arc-it-flow' extension to document
which ARC-IT information flow each data element participates in.
Tooling can walk the module tree and emit coverage reports
against ARC-IT for conformance review.

Copyright (c) 2026.  Distributed under Apache 2.0.
refStd: ARC-IT 9.1 Service Package: Traffic Signal Control (SU01) NTCIP 1202 v03 (functional reference only; no wire compat)

Li, H., A.M. Hainen, J.R. Sturdevant, et al. Indiana Traffic Signal Hi Resolution Data Logger Enumerations. Purdue TPF-5(377), Aug 2020 (doi: 10.5703/1288284316998), supersedes the Nov 2012 original (doi: 10.4231/K4RN35SH)

services.description:
YANG data model for roadside traffic sensors — the radar,
magnetometer, and loop-emulation detector stations that report
per-lane volume, occupancy, speed, and length-based vehicle
classification.

Transport-independent replacement for NTCIP 1209 (Transportation
Sensor Systems), aligned with ARC-IT 9.1 service package TM01
(Infrastructure-Based Traffic Surveillance).  The model is
vendor-neutral: any per-lane aggregating detector — side-fire
radar, magnetometer array, loop emulation — maps onto it.
Vendor-specific capabilities ride in vendor augment modules per
docs/06-extension-model.md, never in this module.

Traffic sensors are pure-observation devices: the only writable
nodes are the lane / classification-bin setup and the reporting
interval.  Bounded values are enforced via YANG `range` because
the bounds are physical rather than operational.

Every numeric leaf carries a `units` statement.  Agents that
report different units MUST convert at the translator boundary;
the wire format is canonical.

Copyright (c) 2026.  Distributed under Apache 2.0.
refStd: ARC-IT 9.1 Service Package TM01 (Infrastructure-Based Traffic Surveillance)
NTCIP 1209 v02 (functional reference only; no wire compatibility)
RFC 7950 section 11 (breaking YANG evolution: identity rename)

RFC 7950 section 11 (breaking YANG evolution: identity rename)
